title: Dropdown
description: A dropdown lets users select a single value from a list of options. It combines a trigger button with a floating option list.
whenToUse: Single selection from a list of 5+ options. When you need to conserve vertical space compared to radio buttons. Form fields where the user picks from a predefined set.
whenNotToUse: For 2–4 options, use radio buttons instead so all options are visible. For text-entry with suggestions, use a combobox/autocomplete. For navigation, use nav or tabs.
acceptanceCriteria: Trigger uses role="combobox" with aria-haspopup="listbox" and aria-expanded reflecting data-open
Listbox uses role="listbox" and options use role="option" with aria-selected on the chosen value
Open / close updates data-open="true|false" so CSS, JS, and a11y stay in sync
Keyboard: Enter/Space, Arrow keys, Home/End, Escape, type-ahead all work as documented
Error state recolors label, trigger border, and helper text; aria-invalid is set
Closing via outside-click, Escape, and selection all return focus to the trigger
Leading icon is OPTIONAL — default examples render WITHOUT a leading icon
When the list opens near the viewport bottom, it flips above the trigger
Disabled trigger does not open and is skipped by Tab

props.name: data-state
props.description: Visual validation state. "error" recolors the label, trigger border, and helper text

props.name: data-open
props.description: Open/closed state of the option list. Reflected from the JS open/close API.

props.name: options
props.description: Array of { value, label, disabled?, leadingIcon? }

props.name: name
props.description: Form field name; required when used inside a <form>

props.name: placeholder
props.description: Trigger text shown when no value is selected

props.name: helperText
props.description: Supporting text below the trigger; recolors with data-state=error

props.name: required
props.description: Renders the required asterisk; sets aria-required

props.name: leadingIcon
props.description: Material Symbols Outlined name rendered inside the trigger before the value. OPTIONAL — leave empty for plain text triggers.

events.name: change
events.description: Fires when the selected value changes

events.name: open
events.description: Fires when the option list opens

events.name: close
events.description: Fires when the option list closes

slots.name: trigger
slots.description: Custom trigger content (advanced; defaults to value/placeholder + chevron)

slots.name: option
slots.description: Custom per-option rendering inside the listbox

states.name: selected
states.description: A value is selected; the trigger displays the chosen label

states.name: open
states.description: data-open="true" — option list visible, chevron rotated

states.name: error
states.description: data-state="error" — label, trigger border, and helper recolor

states.name: empty
states.description: Open list with no matching options — render "No options" placeholder

contentGuidelines: Trigger placeholder: "Select [thing]" not "Choose…". Option labels: sentence case, parallel grammar (all nouns or all verbs). Avoid all-caps. If options have icons, use them consistently — either every option has one or none do.

dos: Use the WAI-ARIA combobox pattern (role=combobox + aria-haspopup=listbox + aria-expanded)
Return focus to the trigger after selection or close
Show the currently selected value in the trigger when closed
donts: Don't use a dropdown for 2–4 options — radio buttons are easier to scan
Don't use a dropdown for free text entry — use a text input or combobox/autocomplete
Don't hide critical information behind a dropdown that users have to open to see
Don't render a leading icon by default — current examples in 0.2 incorrectly use add_circle_outline

keyboard.action: Move focus to / from the trigger
Open the option list when closed; select the focused option when open
Open the list (when closed) and move focus to the first option; while open, move to the next option
Move focus to the previous option (wraps at the top)
Jump to the first / last option
Close the list and return focus to the trigger
Move focus to the next option whose label starts with the typed character

screenReader.announcement: [Label], combobox, [selected value or placeholder], collapsed (or expanded)

screenReader.trigger: Option receives focus inside open listbox
screenReader.announcement: [Option label], [N of M] — selected option is announced as "selected"
Helper text is announced via aria-describedby; consumer should also set aria-invalid="true"

wcag.howMet: role=combobox + aria-haspopup=listbox + aria-expanded follow the WAI-ARIA combobox pattern
Full keyboard support: open/close, navigate, select, type-ahead, escape
Focus moves predictably: into the list on open, back to the trigger on close
Visible focus ring on the trigger and on the focused option
Trigger and option text meet 4.5:1 contrast
Trigger border meets 3:1 against surface; error border uses --uds-color-border-error

knownIssues: Default example uses add_circle_outline as a leading icon, which reads as "add an item" not "select". The leading icon is OPTIONAL and should not be the default.
visualHierarchy: Treat dropdowns as form siblings of text inputs; match label position, helper text style, and required indicator.
densityBehavior: Trigger height matches the text-input field height at the same density. The option list itself uses the comfortable density regardless of the trigger size for readability.
_legacyAccessibilityNotes: Use role="combobox" on the trigger and role="listbox" on the list. aria-expanded announces open/closed state.
